observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per GL and cashbooks; (ii) ensure that only deposits with validated deposit slips be recorded in the cash book and accounting records; and (iii) provide justification on the cash deficit incurred in Trust Fund totalling ₱21,573,608.06.

Municipal Treasurer ensure that all the Special Disbursing Officer maintain cashbook pursuant to Sections 6.1 to 6.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:

a.	Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices coordinate with the suppliers and employees to inform them of the unclaimed payables, otherwise provide certification to the Municipal Accounting Office that there are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion of funds; and

b.	Acting Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that only obligations with approved disbursement voucher and complete supporting documents, and delivery of goods or services rendered be booked as accounts payable pursuant to the prevailing rules and regulation; and (ii) analyze and conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable under the GF and SEF with payables outstanding for more than two years to facilitate the reversion to the unappropriated surplus as mandated in Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed that OIC - Municipal Engineer prepare the monthly monitoring report of the awarded infrastructure PPAs, as well as the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. HRMO prepare the ARA immediately after increase of the salary due to increment and promotion, and forward it to the GSIS on the first week of the month so that the billing of the GSIS will be updated.

Ogbot, inconsistency of completion dates in the Construction of Small-Scale Irrigation Project at Brgy. Tawas, and other noted observation in the implementation of the project; (b) all concerned officials  work hand-in-hand in the prompt implementation and documentation of the projects; (c) BAC Chairperson submit satisfactory explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the procurement of the projects with R-IRR of RA No. 9184;  (d) Municipal Accountant reclassify the recording of the four projects to their appropriate asset and expense accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (e) BAC Chairperson, Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer submit the lacking supporting documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: (a) Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) carefully observe the timeline of issuance and execution of the NOA, PO, Contract Agreement and NTP and its posting to PhilGEPS  set under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) ensure that the bidding documents were properly dated in order to verify the validity of each of the documents submitted by the prospective bidder; (iii) cautiously evaluate the capability of the bidders in the post-qualification stage of the procurement process to avoid continuous request for extension of delivery date and delayed delivery of items procured; (b) Municipal Engineer (i) compute the applicable liquidated damages for the delayed delivery for the purchase of RPC components and late project implementation of expansion of existing Labasan CIS, establishment of school clinic and library; and (ii) ensure compliance with DPWH Department Order No. 101 series of 2005 dated December 22, 2005 regarding the issuance of Certificate of Acceptance;  (c) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) provide acceptable justification on the delayed payments of six contracts entered by the Municipal Government; (ii) recognize in the appropriate asset accounts the water facilities constructed  per COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (iii) submit the lacking documents in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001; and (d) Finance Committee consider the allocation of additional fund to finish the construction of the school clinic and library and attain the objectives of the granting the fund to the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage the active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (c) take appropriate action to fully utilize the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) located in Brgy Sagana;(d) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; and (e) see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and complied with  Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials and employees to provide acceptable justification of the holding of the benchmarking activity in City of Legaspi Camarines Sur and allowing the attendance of job-order and contract of services employees and be allowed to claim per diem for the said activity. Likewise, the Acting Municipal Accountant submit the authority to conduct such activity from DILG and the terminal report of the activity, for audit purposes.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) submit the 150 paid disbursement vouchers totalling ₱53,013,417.15; and (ii) strictly observe and adhere the existing laws and regulation on management of funds and submit to the Audit Team within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month the reports together with the required supporting documents.

We recommend that the: (a) MDRRM Officer, Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer check the correctness of the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. Henceforth, the (i) Municipal Budget Officer ensure that any changes made in the LDRRMFIP be communicated to the MDRRM Officer, and (ii) Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that the Report being certified reflect the true data of MDRRMF; (b)MDRRM Officer evaluate the programs/ projects/activities in the continuing appropriation and special trust fund and make appropriate actions thereto by reprogramming to another MDRRM PPAs that can help in reducing exposure to potential impact of hazards and related disasters in the Municipality; (c)MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson fast-tract the implementation of the programs/projects/activities under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in MDRRM Fund be implemented during the year it was appropriated in order to increase the capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters.

We recommended that: (a) In the planning stage, the Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/projects/activities; (ii) evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; and (iii) ensure that the implementing departments achieve a consensus on all aspects of the projects (iv) predetermine beforehand all issues that could be encountered regarding the implementation of the projects to effectively address the same to ensure the smooth implementation of the projects and; (b) The Municipal Engineer fast track the preparation of the plans and strictly follow the planned implementation period as per duly approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform the:(a)Medical Officer IV of RURCH instruct the personnel in-charge in PhilHealth reimbursements to provide the Accounting Office a copy of the reimbursements of hospital bills sent to PhilHealth; (b) Acting Municipal Accountant adopt accrual basis of accounting and institute a system of control relative to the recording of reimbursements from PhilHealth and maintain subsidiary ledger for the same; (c) In-charge of PhilHealth reimbursements in RURCH notify the Municipal Treasurer once Auto-Credit Payment Notice (ACPN) was given by PhilHealth; and (d) Municipal Treasurer once ACPN was given, immediately check their bank account if the amount was credited for her, to issue Official Receipt in compliance with Item V.F of PhilHealth Circular No. 2017-0020 effective September 1, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:(a) MPDC  spearhead the updating and maintenance of GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;(b) MSWDO/GFP  (i) observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-001 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission of GPB to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and subsequent review and endorsement by the Provincial DILG; and (ii) comply with the revisions requested by the Provincial DILG, if any before securing their Certificate of Endorsement; (c) Finance Committee  allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (d) Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD programs, projects and activities are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; and (e)GAD TWG (i) convene during the GAD planning and budgeting process to facilitate the gender mainstreaming in the LGU, (ii) gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of LGU programs and projects through attribution using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool; and (iii) strictly comply with Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 to ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Account agreed to (i)conduct thorough review and analysis of Due to NGAs account under the General Fund and Trust Fund and reclassify the funds that do not came from the National Government Agencies to Trust Liabilities account pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribing the Revised chart of Accounts for Local Government Units;(ii)exert effort to collate details about the long outstanding Due to NGAs account in order to make necessary actions regarding these unutilized government funds; and (iii)refund the excess amount of ₱201,152.99 from the fully implemented projects to the source agencies pursuant  to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Engineer submit written explanation on the delay incurred in the implementation of the projects and why the projects were improperly maintained after its completion; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant remit to the DILG Regional Office the unutilized balance of ₱301,521.88 pursuant to Section XII, Item A.1(c) of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2015-74 dated July 14, 2015; and (c) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson explain the other observations noted and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.

No. 9003; (e) initiate the passage of Anti-Littering policy ordinances as provided by Section 48 of R.A. No. 9003; and (f) see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and complied with  Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer immediately submit the original copy of the 445 paid disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱78,369,835.80 and nine liquidation reports (report of disbursements) for the cash advances of the disbursing officer totaling ₱378,574.30 together with all the supporting documents to COA Office for verification and audit purposes, to avoid suspension in audit. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative the submission of reports and accounts to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the: (a) MPDC and MDRRMO provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; and (ii) specific period of implementation for which the PPAs of MDRRM will be implemented; (b) MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer (i) review the unobligated balances under the continuing appropriation of LDRRM Fund  and reprogram to other MDRRM PPAs, or if not, revert to general fund for appropriation to other projects; and (ii) provide a detailed list of the projects under the continuing appropriation; and (c) MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer fast-tract the implementation of the programs/projects/activities under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented.

We recommended that:(a) In the planning stage, the Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i)consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/ projects/ activities, and (ii)evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; (b)the Municipal Engineer ensure collaboration with other concerned municipal officials and employees to minimize frequent revisions of the POW and periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus; and (c) The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the: (a) Management consider a more aggressive estimation of revenue targets taking into consideration that their collection from the past years; (b)ICO - Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to work hand-in-hand to update their records especially those properties with multiple owners, which could include actual field verification of the properties and appropriate updating of records accordingly;(c)ICO - Municipal Treasurer devise various strategies and activities to facilitate effective revenue collection by: (i) assigning more personnel in delivering the notice of delinquencies, with specific instructions to serves notices to actual owner of the property or to the actual occupants and institute follow up actions in case of failure to reply; (ii) comprehensive tax information campaign; and (iii) integration of tax mapping process into the iTAX system. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairperson to provide explanation on the above noted deficiencies. Likewise, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairperson submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting and the Treasurer’s Offices to adhere strictly to the provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative the submission of reports and accounts to the Office of the Auditor.

Municipal Accounting Office use the list as prescribed by 16.1.1 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9433 instead of requiring preparation of Accomplishment Reports to substantiate the exposure to hazards and occupational risks of the concerned public social workers and public social and development workers.

We recommended that:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra efforts to establish her PPE Ledger Cards/list of properties, verify the status of the agency asset which have been under construction for three years now and the documents pertaining to such construction and the veracity of the inclusion of Travelling Expenses amounting to ₱49,420.00 to IT Equipment and Software account balance, effect the necessary adjustment, if any, and apply the correct residual value and depreciation to all depreciable properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned recipients of the iPads to submit written explanations why their acquisition of expensive gadget with 64 GB should not be disallowed, otherwise, the claim for the payment of Ipad will be disallowed and refunded by the concerned officials/ employees.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (1) require the BAC, General Service Officer and Accountant to submit the lacking necessary documentary requirements stated above and (2) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a copy to COA Auditor of the Journal Entry Voucher which will adjust the Due to Other NGAs account and recognize the asset - Construction in Progress-Roads, Highways and Bridges both amounting to ₱12,705,865.67 to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements. We recommended that the Municipality refrain from granting communication allowance to the MLGOO and to its officials and employees, rather set a policy regarding the payment of communication expense identifying the allowed officials and employees, the amount to be granted and the plan to be used in order to identify the most favorable option for the municipality’s scarce resources. No. 7160 for Discretionary Fund in the appropriation and spending of the same. In case the medicines are not included in the PNF Manual, prepare written request with justification and secure approval from the Director, National Center for Pharmaceutical Access and Management.

observations.recommendation:

Furthermore, require concerned Municipal Officials to submit the Liquidation Report and strictly follow the guidelines for excessive, extravagant and unnecessary expenses cited in COA Circular No. 2012-003 and properly plan each activity so they can have savings for important projects/activities beneficial to most constituents or the minorities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Reports of Disbursements for the cash advances totaling ₱328,000.00 were submitted on October 17, 2013. 

Moreover, the Municipal Mayor mentioned that the conduct of festivals, cultural and historical events, is included in the Municipality’s Executive and Legislative Agenda (ELA) since they never treated it as expenses but rather an investment for tourism promotion and development. Likewise, he assured compliance in the recommendations on celebration of Sulyog Festival.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage the active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (c) take appropriate action to fully utilize the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) located in Brgy Sagana; (d) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; and (e) see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and complied with Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO coordinated with the NSWMC last May 26, 2017. MENRO coordinated with the concerned agencies but they suggested to convene the Solid Waste Management Board for the preparation of Action Plan for integration to LGU plans for funding. PPE for solid waste collectors will be procured on the 3rd quarter of the year.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to facilitate the compliance of RA No. 9003. No sufficient funds were allocated for the current year since MENRO is a newly established department in the municipality.

No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative the submission of reports and accounts to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the (a) Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of
cash accounts per GL and cashbooks; (b) Municipal Treasure maintain and update
cashbooks of all bank accounts to monitor daily transactions and cancel the
unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accountant with the original DVs and its
supporting documents; and (c) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) conduct detailed
review and checking of bank records/reports and request for the necessary
documents to support the recording of the reconciling items; (ii) exert effort to
substantiate the identified reconciling items and after which prepare the necessary
adjustments; and (iii) review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those
checks without identified payees and afterward adjust those stale checks.

We recommended that the (a) Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer trace/ investigate the negative balance of the Cash – Local Treasury
account under Trust Fund and effect in the books the necessary adjusting entries, if
warrant; and (b) Acting Municipal Accountant identify the discrepancy between the
Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledger of the Cash – Local Treasury Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) concerned
officials and employees settle the outstanding cash advances as of December 31,
2018; (b) Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that cash
advances be only granted to employees duly authorized/designated as Special
Disbursing Officer who is properly bonded pursuant to with Section 4.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 97-002, Section 305(f) of RA 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 2-2009;
and (c) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) avoid the grant of cash advances to JO,
COS and NGAs personnel with no employer-employee relationship with MGB in
order to avoid the possibility of non-liquidation in case of non-renewal of
contract/job-order or reassignment of the personnel; (ii) encourage the JO and COS
personnel to reimburse the amount incurred during official travel instead of having
cash advances; (iii) explain and submit legal basis on the provision of cash
incentives to PNP and 4th Platoon personnel; (iv) immediately submit the original
copy of the 151 paid disbursement vouchers totaling P2,506,782.84, liquidation
reports of the three employees with cash advances totaling P35,940.00 together with
all the supporting documents, and the lacking documents on the liquidation of cash
advances of the Tourism Focal Person to COA Office for verification and audit
purposes.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant enforce the
liquidation of the assistance granted from 2018 and prior years totalling
P2,137,963.46 to various barangays and schools in the Municipality. Henceforth,
ensure that assistance granted to various barangays was covered by a Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA) for them to be accountable on the assistance granted to them,
and for the Municipal Government to identify if the assistance was utilized in
accordance with the intended purpose.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and General Service
Officer allot time and effort to coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to
facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of
actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; (b) Municipal
Accountant (i) analyze the nature of the transactions of the semi-expendable
supplies and property and misclassified accounts, and make an adjustment to
properly classify the items to a more appropriate account; and (ii) record in the
Accounting Books the 75% of the infrastructure assets previously transferred to
Registry of Public Infrastructures (RPI); (c) GSO: (i) account all determined
unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other required supporting
documents for disposal; (ii) reconcile the RPCLRN with the Registry of Public
Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; and (iii) collate the documents
of the land owned by the Municipal Government that remained uncovered by a
Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT), and process the same; (d) Municipal Engineer
maintain road map and provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
and (e) Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis
to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of
48
each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real
properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.

We recommended that the General Service Officer prepare complete list of
insurable properties with its value, if determinable, to facilitate the processing of
insurance with GSIS; and the Municipal Budget Officer include in the ensuing
budget preparation the costs of the insurance premiums of all the insurable
properties of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer (i) compute the liquidated
damages for the delayed implementation of the project, taking into consideration
the days of the invalid suspension orders issued. Henceforth, ensure that suspension
orders to be issued are compliant with the provision of Item 9.1 of Annex E of the R-
IRR of RA No. 9184; and (ii) provide justification on the issued variation order
issued for the Road Opening of Local Access Roads in Sitio Gabayno, Brgy. Ogbot,
inconsistency of completion dates in the Construction of Small-Scale Irrigation
Project at Brgy. Tawas, and other noted observation in the implementation of the
project; (b) all concerned officials work hand-in-hand in the prompt
implementation and documentation of the projects; (c) BAC Chairman submit
satisfactory explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the procurement of the
projects with R-IRR of RA No. 9184; (d) Municipal Accountant reclassify the
recording of the four projects to their appropriate asset and expense accounts in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (e) BAC
Chairman, Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer submit the
lacking supporting documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: (a) Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) (i)
carefully observe the timeline of issuance and execution of the NOA, PO, Contract
Agreement and NTP and its posting to PhilGEPS set under the Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184; (ii) ensure that the bidding documents were properly dated in order to
verify the validity of each of the documents submitted by the prospective bidder;
(iii) cautiously evaluate the capability of the bidders in the post-qualification stage
of the procurement process to avoid continuous request for extension of delivery
date and delayed delivery of items procured; (b) Municipal Engineer (i) compute the
applicable liquidated damages for the delayed delivery for the purchase of RPC
components and late project implementation of expansion of existing Labasan CIS,
establishment of school clinic and library; and (ii) ensure compliance with DPWH
Department Order No. 101 series of 2005 dated December 22, 2005 regarding the
issuance of Certificate of Acceptance; (c) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) provide
acceptable justification on the delayed payments of six contracts entered by the
Municipal Government; (ii) recognize in the appropriate asset accounts the water
facilities constructed per COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
(iii) submit the lacking documents in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001; and
(d) Finance Committee consider the allocation of additional fund to finish the
construction of the school clinic and library and attain the objectives of the granting
the fund to the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials
and employees to provide acceptable justification of the holding of the
benchmarking activity in City of Legaspi Camarines Sur and allowing the
attendance of job-order and contract of services employees and be allowed to claim
per diem for the said activity. Likewise, the Acting Municipal Accountant submit
the authority to conduct such activity from DILG and the terminal report of the
activity, for audit purposes.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i)
submit the 150 paid disbursement vouchers totalling P53,013,417.15; and (ii) strictly
observe and adhere the existing laws and regulation on management of funds and
submit to the Audit Team within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month the
reports together with the required supporting documents.

We recommended that: (a) In the planning stage, the Local Development
Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and weather
factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/projects/activities;
(ii) evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund
needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; and (iii) ensure that
the implementing departments achieve a consensus on all aspects of the projects (iv)
predetermine beforehand all issues that could be encountered regarding the
implementation of the projects to effectively address the same to ensure the smooth
implementation of the projects and; (b) The Municipal Engineer fast track the
preparation of the plans and strictly follow the planned implementation period as
per duly approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform the:(a)Medical Officer
IV of RURCH instruct the personnel in-charge in PhilHealth reimbursements to
provide the Accounting Office a copy of the reimbursements of hospital bills sent to
PhilHealth; (b) Acting Municipal Accountant adopt accrual basis of accounting and
institute a system of control relative to the recording of reimbursements from
PhilHealth and maintain subsidiary ledger for the same; (c) In-charge of PhilHealth
reimbursements in RURCH notify the Municipal Treasurer once Auto-Credit
Payment Notice (ACPN) was given by PhilHealth; and (d) Municipal Treasurer
once ACPN was given, immediately check their bank account if the amount was
credited for her, to issue Official Receipt in compliance with Item V.F of PhilHealth
Circular No. 2017-0020 effective September 1, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:(a) MPDC
spearhead the updating and maintenance of GAD Database to serve as basis for
gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;(b)
MSWDO/GFP (i) observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided
by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-001 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate
timely submission of GPB to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and
subsequent review and endorsement by the Provincial DILG; and (ii) comply with
the revisions requested by the Provincial DILG, if any before securing their
Certificate of Endorsement; (c) Finance Committee allocate at least 5% of the total
annual budget for implementation of GAD-related activities as required under
Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (d) Heads of
implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD programs, projects and
activities are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; and (e)GAD
TWG (i) convene during the GAD planning and budgeting process to facilitate the
gender mainstreaming in the LGU, (ii) gradually increase the gender-responsiveness
of LGU programs and projects through attribution using the Harmonized Gender and
Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool; and (iii) strictly comply with Section 36
of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 to ensure that the
programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that
the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other
assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant (i) prepare a
schedule of the unremitted Due to GSIS of P1,043,746.00 and coordinate with GSIS
Office if these can still be remitted, if not, refund the withheld personal contribution
to respective employees while the government share (employer share) be returned to
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund; and (ii) identify those personnel
whom the P15,001.31 were withheld, and if this cannot be remitted to PhilHealth,
refund the same to the concerned personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal ICO-Treasurer: ensure that the collections of the collectors are within the maximum cash accountability allowed in their fidelity bond, if possible, coordinate with the Bureau of Treasury for the increase in their fidelity bonds; Likewise, ensure that non-bonded employees are properly supervise by the bonded employee and install control measures in the collection of the non-bonded employees; discontinue the practice of keeping large amount of collections in their hand/possession to avoid possible loss and misappropriation. Likewise, deposit the collections intact every day or as frequently as possible; require the Liquidating Officer to verify whether the collections pertaining the used and all issued accountable forms are remitted before new series of receipts will be issued to collectors; and submit comments/justification on the above noted observation in the collection and deposit, and issuance and use of accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: The ICO- Municipal Treasurer proposed to increase the budget for the fidelity bond of employees accountable in handling collections of the Municipality. The OIC- Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendations of the COA.
observations.reason: Due to the distance of the Municipal Government’s AGDB and lack of service vehicle, large amount of collection were still on hand of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.managementAction: The concerned employees committed to comply with the recommendations of the COA. BAC Chairman already submitted the documents.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to review the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal School Board and Municipal Budget Officer follow the provision of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. of 2017 in preparing the SEF budget; and (b) Municipal Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant require the concerned DepEd personnel to submit satisfactory justifications on the observations noted, to avoid disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer allocate the required 1% of their IRA for LCPC programs/projects/activities as mandated by Section 15 of RA 9344; and (b) the MSWDO abide with the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund as provided by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Budget Officer and MSWDO ensures that programs/projects/activities that address the concerns of senior citizens and persons with disability be included in the Annual Budget of the Municipality, if possible appropriate at least 1% of the total appropriation pursuant to DILG MC No. 2014-59 dated May 9, 2014.

observations.reason: Lack of manpower to prepare and monitor senior citizens and PWD programs/projects activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) MPDC and MDRRMO provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; and (ii) specific period of implementation for which the PPAs of MDRRM will be implemented; (b) MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer (i) review the unobligated balances under the continuing appropriation of LDRRM Fund and reprogram to other MDRRM PPAs, or if not, revert to general fund for appropriation to other projects; and (c) MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer fast-tract the implementation of the programs/projects/activities under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees committed to comply with the recommendations of COA.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation of Program of Works, and some projects are not priority to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) In the planning stage, the Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/projects/activities; (ii) evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; and (iii) ensure that the implementing departments achieve a consensus on all aspects of the projects (iv) predetermine beforehand all issues that could be encountered regarding the implementation of the projects to effectively address the same to ensure the smooth implementation of the projects and; (b) The Municipal Engineer fast track the preparation of the plans and strictly follow the planned implementation period as per duly approved Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Local Chief Executive formed the Municipal Project Implementation Team (MPIT) to monitor all the unimplemented projects of the municipality. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to monitor the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC and Municipal Agriculturist provide sufficient justification in the planning stage of fishing paraphernalia; and the Municipal Agriculturist immediately distribute the fishing paraphernalia as planned and exert effort to encourage the intended beneficiaries to register their boat being one of the requirements set in the project proposal. If the beneficiaries refused, the Municipality need to consider other options in order to distribute the fishing paraphernalia accordingly to avoid the risk of possible deterioration and loss of the gears.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consider a more aggressive estimation of revenue targets taking into consideration that their collection from the past years; (b) ICO - Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to work hand-in-hand to update their records especially those properties with multiple owners, which could include actual field verification of the properties and appropriate updating of records accordingly; (c) ICO- Municipal Treasurer devise various strategies and activities to facilitate effective revenue collection by: (i) assigning more personnel in delivering the notice of delinquencies, with specific instructions to serves notices to actual owner of the property or to the actual occupants and institute follow up actions in case of failure to reply; (ii) comprehensive tax information campaign; and (iii) integration of tax mapping process into the iTAX system. Moreover, implement remedy provided under Section 256 of RA 7160 and explore all possible remedies as provided under RA 7160 and relevant regulations to collect those delinquent accounts for more than five years; (d) ICO – Municipal Treasurer submit a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year in his jurisdiction to their local sanggunian in accordance with Section 269 of RA 7160; and (e) OIC – Municipal Accountant be guided with COA Circular 2015-009 in classification of accounts pertaining to real property.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Treasurer is undertaking measures to formulate strategies on how to increase the collection efficiency of the Municipality. The OIC- Municipal Accountant committed to review and analyze the transactions to reconcile the records in their RPT and make necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer is undertaking measures to formulate strategies on how to increase the collection efficiency of the Municipality. observations.managementAction: The Municipality still maintained funds in Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) - Calapan.
observations.reason: Only 67% of the total funds of the Municipality was deposited in LBP – Pinamalayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: set a policy regarding the payment of communication expense identifying the allowed officials and employees, the amount to be granted and the plan to be used in order to identify the most favorable option for the municipality’s scarce resources. Furthermore, require complete documentation for communication allowance; require complete documentation for communication expense claimed by department heads, issuance of Acknowledgement Receipt for those mobile phones, and recognize the asset that pertains to the mobile phone units availed by the officials and employees concerned. Finally, require the concerned department heads to refund the “Pasa load” granted to other people which can be considered personal in nature and no longer an official matter.
observations.managementAction: A guideline has been set in the Annual Budget of the Municipality for the communication allowance.
observations.reason: The amount of “pasa load” is still not refunded by the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees.
